description_long: This object consists of four books arranged upright on a rectangular wooden surface. The books vary in size and color, with covers in shades of red, black, and brown, and some showing visible wear and frayed edges. One book is open, displaying its cream-colored pages, while the others are closed, with one standing vertically and another lying flat. The wooden base is slightly worn and has a natural, unfinished look with visible grain and a few dark spots. The books' spines and covers show signs of age, with faded colors and some discoloration. The overall scene evokes a sense of vintage or well-used personal items, possibly found on a street-side book stall or in a public reading area.
description: A group of four worn books in various colors and sizes, arranged upright on a rustic wooden surface. The books show visible signs of age, with one open and the rest closed.
description_view_0: From this view, the open book with a reddish-brown cover is prominent, with its pages fanned out. The red and black books stand upright behind it, and the wooden surface is clearly visible.
description_view_1: This angle shows the spines and edges of the books, with the open book's pages facing forward and the red book's spine visible. The black book appears thinner from this perspective.
description_view_2: Here, the front covers of the books are visible, including the open book's inner pages and the red book's cover. The wooden surface appears more rectangular and the arrangement of the books is clearer.
description_view_3: This view highlights the backs and tops of the books, with the open book's cover and pages visible from behind. The arrangement of the books on the wooden surface is evident.
attribute_spine_design: The spines are mostly plain, with one red spine featuring faint horizontal lines and possible faded text. The other spines are solid colors with no visible decorative elements.
